# Install the shell hook twice, assert the fence block exists exactly once and
# responds correctly to --no-alias-kubectl.

exec kcm install-shell-hook --shell=zsh --rc=$WORK/.zshrc
stdout 'installed zsh hook'
grep '>>> kubeconfig-manager shell hook >>>' $WORK/.zshrc
grep 'alias kubectl=' $WORK/.zshrc

# Re-run replaces the block in place (not appended) and honors --no-alias-kubectl.
exec kcm install-shell-hook --shell=zsh --rc=$WORK/.zshrc --no-alias-kubectl
stdout 'updated zsh hook'
grep '>>> kubeconfig-manager shell hook >>>' $WORK/.zshrc
! grep 'alias kubectl=' $WORK/.zshrc

# Uninstall wipes the block.
exec kcm uninstall-shell-hook --shell=zsh --rc=$WORK/.zshrc
stdout 'removed kubeconfig-manager hook'
! grep '>>> kubeconfig-manager shell hook >>>' $WORK/.zshrc
